rationale: The talk is an argument in human voice, not a data or product walkthrough — Style A's restraint, serif hierarchy, and whitespace carry an argument better than a hard grid.
Audience is a craft conference in a dark room: warm paper + one ink accent reads as considered, on-theme with the talk's own thesis (subtraction, restraint).
Only one genuinely numeric beat (the worked contrast) — not enough data density to want Swiss's grid-and-KPI machinery.
The brief leaned editorial; nothing in the content overrides that, so the lean is honored with a stated reason rather than reflexively.

rejected.why: Swiss international (sans-serif, single accent, hard 12-col grid, extreme contrast) is built for factual / product / data-dense decks. Here it would fight the talk's narrative voice and over-formalize a 9-slide argument — the wrong tool for THIS brief.

theme_note: One preset chosen (no custom hex beyond the preset's own tokens), per the skill's 'pick a preset, not a colour' rule. The accent is the single anchor carried into assemble_deck and social_cover.
steer_hint: This is the headline steer node: switch style to B and rhythm_table, select_layouts, assemble_deck (and the cover) all go stale — run `flowtrace show --downstream choose_style`.
source: judgment per op7418/guizang-ppt-skill SKILL.md Step 1 · 需求澄清 (first question: Style A or B) + the two systems' guiding principles (Style A editorial × e-ink vs Style B Swiss international).
